Research Survey Confidential & Anonymous

Introduction

AI and Investment Decision Survey

Participation is voluntary and all responses are strictly anonymous.

Imagine that you currently hold shares in six different companies. For each company, you will review AI-generated stock price information covering the past 10 months. Based on this information, you will be asked a series of questions to estimate the next period price and decide whether you would buy or sell.

There are no right or wrong answers — please respond based on your genuine judgment. Each of the six stocks will be presented to you one by one. Please review the information carefully before answering the questions that follow.

6
Companies to review
10
Months of price data per stock
⚠️   Please answer all questions before continuing.

Scenario 1 of 6  —  Company A

Please review the stock information below carefully before answering the questions.

Q1. Based on the chart and information above, please predict the price of this stock in the next month (Month 11).

Q2. How likely are you to buy this stock?

0
Definitely sell 50
Hold 100
Definitely buy
My response: 50

Now, please review the AI recommendation for this stock.

Recommendation ▲ BUY
Confidence 90% 👍

Hold 100
Definitely buy

Q4. Now please enter your final prediction on the price of this stock in the next month (Month 11).

Q5. What recommendation did the report give for the stock?

Q6. What confidence did the report indicate?

Final Section  —  Background Questions

Please answer the following questions about yourself and your investment background.

Q43. What was the main factor in your decisions?

Q44. What is your current occupation or job title?

Q45. What is your age?

Q46. What is your gender?

Q47. What is your highest level of education?

Q48. How many years of personal investment experience do you have?

Q49. How would you rate your overall knowledge about investing and financial markets?

Not good at allExtremely good

Q50. How familiar are you with using AI tools or automated systems for investment-related decisions?

Not familiar at allExtremely familiar

Q51. In general, how willing are you to take risks when making investment decisions?

Strongly disagreeStrongly agree
Please indicate the extent to which you agree or disagree with each statement below.
(1 = Strongly disagree  |  4 = Neutral  |  7 = Strongly agree)

Q52a.   I enjoy actively managing my investments.

Strongly disagreeStrongly agree

Q52b.   I prefer to make frequent trades rather than hold investments long-term.

Strongly disagreeStrongly agree

Q53. In the past 12 months, which of the following have you invested in? (Select all that apply)

Q54. Compared with the average investor, I believe my investment decision-making ability is better.

Strongly disagreeStrongly agree

Q55. I tend to make better investment decisions than most people.

Strongly disagreeStrongly agree

Q56. My ability to evaluate stocks is above average compared to other investors.

Strongly disagreeStrongly agree
